issue_title: Invalid xkb options result in unusable keyboard and therefore no way to exit sway
issue_body:
* Sway Version: `1.1-rc1-4-g8c8e6a6c (May 11 2019, branch 'HEAD')`

I was trying to set up a local keyboard layout. I made a mistake and confused `en` with `us`, etc. This resulted in a sway instance that I was not available to terminate (no `Ctrl-Alt-Fn` switching, no `win-shift-e`, etc). I understand that it is a config error, but it's a pity that I had to power-cycle the whole box.

- A minimal config file:
```
set $mod Mod4

input type:keyboard {
        xkb_layout "en,cs"
        xkb_variant ",qwerty"
        xkb_options "grp:alt_shift_toggle"
}

bindsym $mod+Return exec konsole

bindsym $mod+Shift+q kill

bindsym $mod+Shift+e exec swaynag -t warning -m 'You pressed the exit shortcut. Do you really want to exit sway? This will end your Wayland session.' -b 'Yes, exit sway' 'swaymsg exit'
```
(Some) invalid configurations result in an error message like this in the debug log:
```
xkbcommon: ERROR: Couldn't find file "symbols/en" in include paths
xkbcommon: ERROR: 1 include paths searched:
xkbcommon: ERROR:       /usr/share/X11/xkb
xkbcommon: ERROR: 1 include paths could not be added:
xkbcommon: ERROR:       /home/jkt/.xkb
xkbcommon: ERROR: Abandoning symbols file "(unnamed)"
xkbcommon: ERROR: Failed to compile xkb_symbols
xkbcommon: ERROR: Failed to compile keymap
2019-05-13 13:15:28 - [sway-9999/sway/input/keyboard.c:515] cannot configure keyboard: keymap does not exist
```

- Expected behavior:
  - In a perfect world, I would love to see a user-visible error message (similar to what I get when I reload config on the fly and there's a config schema error).
  - Alternatively, as a minimum, make sure that keyboard is still available. Default to an english layout "as if no config was present".

In case someone ends up here by a random web search, this is the correct config stanza:
```
input type:keyboard {
        xkb_layout "us,cz"
        xkb_variant ",qwerty"
        xkb_options "grp:alt_shift_toggle"
}
```
pr_body:
Closes #4148 (this is the error part; see #4153 for something usable)

**Just the first commit**: basic invalid keymap prevention

Before the delta input config is stored, this attempts to compile a
keymap with it. If the keymap fails to compile, then the first line of
the xkbcommon log entry will be included with a `CMD_FAILURE`, the
entire xkbcommon log entry will be included in the sway error log, and
the delta will not be stored.

This will only handle basic issues such as a layouts not existing. This
will NOT catch more complex issues such as when a variant does
exist, but not for the given layout (ex: `azerty` is a valid variant,
but the `us` layout does not have a `azerty` variant).

**Both commits combined**: full invalid keymap protection

This allows for an optional validation stage of the input config before
storing it. Currently, the only validation performed is a valid xkb
configuration. The validation stage will do a dry run of the storage
process and make sure that storing the input config will not result in
any invalid xkb configurations. If there are any xkb keymap compilation
errors, the input config will not be stored, the full xkbcommon log
will be rewritten to the sway log, and the first line will be stored in
the error variable passed to store_input_config. The error is then used
to provide a little bit of information for the CMD_FAILURE.
